body:
biblicallyaccurateromina:

I pass as a girl far better than I ever passed as a boy. And I don’t mean in my appearance.

Growing up a trans woman (and as an egg), people could tell there was something off about me. That I didn’t fit in. That I wasn’t ‘one of the guys.’

No matter how hard I tried, the mask was always transparent. They saw my face beneath it, even when I didn’t see it myself.

Transitioning doesn’t just mean hormones and skirts. It means passing as yourself, instead of a thing pretending to be you.
